audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Mark's opening, euangelion, Isaiah/Malachi wilderness messenger composite, John, baptism, Spirit descent, wilderness testing, basileia/metanoia proclamation, first disciples, Capernaum authority, unclean spirit language, Simon's mother-in-law, healing summary, prayer withdrawal, and skin-disease cleansing.

verses.restored: Beginning of the good message of Jesus Messiah, Son of the Divine.
As it has been written in Isaiah the prophet: Look, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way.
A voice of one crying in the wilderness: Prepare the way of the Lord; make his paths straight.
John came baptizing in the wilderness and proclaiming a baptism of turning/realignment for release of sins/misalignments.
All the Judean region and all Jerusalem were going out to him, and they were baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins/misalignments.
John was clothed with camel hair and a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
He proclaimed, saying, After me comes the one stronger than I; I am not worthy to stoop down and loosen the strap of his sandals.
I baptized you in water, but he will baptize you in holy Spirit.
In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
Immediately, coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens being torn open and the Spirit descending into him like a dove.
A voice came from the heavens: You are my beloved Son; in you I delight.
Immediately the Spirit drives him out into the wilderness.
He was in the wilderness forty days, being tested by the adversarial one; he was with the wild animals, and the messengers served him.
After John was handed over, Jesus came into Galilee proclaiming the good message of the Divine,
and saying, The time has been fulfilled, and the reign of the Divine has drawn near; turn/realign and trust in the good message.
Passing along beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew, Simon's brother, casting a net in the sea, for they were fishers.
Jesus said to them, Come after me, and I will make you become fishers of people.
Immediately they left the nets and followed him.
Going on a little farther, he saw James son of Zebedee and John his brother, who also were in the boat mending the nets.
Immediately he called them, and leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers, they went away after him.
They went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath he entered the synagogue and taught.
They were struck with wonder at his teaching, for he was teaching them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
Immediately there was in their synagogue a man in an unclean spirit, and he cried out,
What to us and to you, Jesus Nazarene? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are: the Holy One of the Divine.
Jesus rebuked it, saying, Be silent, and come out from him.
The unclean spirit convulsed him, cried with a loud voice, and came out from him.
They were all amazed, so that they discussed among themselves, saying, What is this? A new teaching with authority: he commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him.
Immediately the report about him went out everywhere into the whole surrounding region of Galilee.
Immediately, going out from the synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
Simon's mother-in-law was lying down with fever, and immediately they spoke to him about her.
He came, raised her by taking her hand, and the fever left her; and she served them.
When evening came, when the sun set, they brought to him all who were sick and those troubled by unclean spirits.
The whole city was gathered at the door.
He healed many who were sick with various diseases and cast out many unclean spirits; he did not permit the spirits to speak, because they knew him.
Very early, while it was still dark, he rose, went out, and departed to a deserted place; there he prayed.
Simon and those with him searched for him.
They found him and said to him, Everyone is seeking you.
He said to them, Let us go elsewhere, into the neighboring towns, so that I may proclaim there also; for this is why I came out.
He went into all Galilee, proclaiming in their synagogues and casting out unclean spirits.
A man with a skin disease came to him, pleading and kneeling, saying, If you are willing, you are able to cleanse me.
Moved with compassion, Jesus stretched out his hand, touched him, and said, I am willing; be cleansed.
Immediately the skin disease left him, and he was cleansed.
Strictly warning him, he immediately sent him away,
and said to him, See that you say nothing to anyone, but go show yourself to the priest and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.
But he went out and began to proclaim many things and spread the word, so that Jesus could no longer enter a city openly, but stayed outside in deserted places; and they came to him from everywhere.
